notes: The doctrinally important compound is kāmātman — not 'desirous' (which everyone is) but 'whose ātman/very nature is constituted as desire'. The engineering layer preserves this constitutive force precisely: 'the speakers are kāmātman — those whose constitutive identity is desire.' The trajectory bhoga-aiśvarya-gati is named as 'real but bounded' — exactly what Shankara reads ('these are real attainments at that level; the error is treating them as ultimate') and what Ramanuja reads ('the same rituals performed without attachment to fruit and as worship remain valid means'). Kriyā-viśeṣa-bahulā ('elaborate ritual specification') is preserved with engineering parallel that does not erase the original. Janma-karma-phala-pradām (yielding rebirth as the karma-fruit) is preserved as 'rebirth into the same shape, not transformation of shape' — capturing the saṃsāric trajectory point. PASS.
